title: cuminia eriantha
intro: Cuminia eriantha is a plant species, also known as Wild Cumin or Ceylon Cumin, belonging to the Rutaceae family and native to Sri Lanka. The plant's medicinal properties include its essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Cuminia eriantha include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Cuminia eriantha include tea, powder, and essential oil, which can be consumed or applied topically under medical supervision.
